title: Common-Subexpression Scheduling for a Multi-Output Polynomial Kernel
strategies: trivial: rebuild every monomial per term with no sharing (reproduces the grader's naive baseline, ~0.1)
greedy: compute each DISTINCT monomial once and reuse it across all targets
strong: canonicalize monomials and share leading SUB-PRODUCTS via a prefix trie so common partial products are built once across all targets
invalid: emit a well-formed circuit that computes the wrong function (all-zero outputs) -> exact-equivalence check fails, score 0
